verses:
esa mama hṛdi mandire śrī⋅gaura⋅hari,
pañca⋅tattvarūpe tomāya dekhaba āmi nayana bhari

(tumi) vraje chile vrajera rākhāla (gaura he)
nade ha’le dvijera chāoyāla,
tumi kālo chile gaura ha’le
ki vāsanā mane kari

kālo’rūpa sājita bhāla, gaurarūpa tā ha’teo bhāla
(ogo) śacīra nandana ekhana tomāra e sannyāsīrūpa
herite nāri

gale vanamālā chila (gaura he) ekhana ke tā keḍe nila,
tulasīra mālā galāya dila ūrdhavapuṇḍra nāsā bhari

tumi kāre dile mohana cūḍā kāre dile pītadhaḍā,
māthā neḍā geruyā vasana parale tumi kemana kari

rādhā rādhā rādhā bali’ bājāte mohana muralī,
muralī vinā ai vadane bala’cha sadāi hari hari

(he) nadera cā̐da nade kare, esa mama hṛda mājhāre,
tomāra gaurarūpa nayane here mānava janama saphala kari
en_translation:
O Lord Gaura⋅hari! Please come into the temple of my heart. I will gaze upon You in the form of the five spiritual truths to my heart's content.

In Vraja, You were a cowherd boy, O Lord Gaura! In Nadīyā, You became the child of a brāhmaṇa. You were dark-complexioned, and now You have become golden—what secret desire do You hold in Your heart?

The dark form of Lord Kṛṣṇa was beautiful, but Your golden form is even more beautiful than that. O Son of Mother Śacī! Now I cannot bear to see Your form as a renunciant.

Around Your neck was a garland of forest flowers—who has taken that away now? They have placed a necklace of Tulasī beads around Your neck and decorated Your nose with the sacred markings of a devotee.

To whom did You give Your enchanting crown? To whom did You give Your yellow garments? How is it that You have a shaven head and wear the saffron garments of a sannyāsī?

Chanting "Rādhā! Rādhā! Rādhā!" You used to play Your enchanting flute, but now, without a flute, You are always chanting "Hari! Hari!" with that very mouth.

O Moon of Nadīyā! Please come into the core of my heart. By seeing Your golden form with my own eyes, I will make my human birth successful.
